entry.title: Re: Is there any API can be used to post Goods Receipt?
entry.content: What about Private Cloud?

entry.title: How to fetch Country of Origin from MARA to MCH1 in batch classification?
entry.content:
Hi Guru,
Country of Origin is maintain at material's level with class type 023.
During batch creation in 101 for GR from PO, the COO still keeps with the material.
How can we copy it to batch master classification?

E.g. How can pass the COO "CN" in MARA on to LOBM_HERKL in MCH1?

I assume deviation is an approach. 
Can we get it done by object dependence?
Thank you,
Sally

entry.title: Re: How to identify vulnerabilities according to our SAP...
entry.content:
see KBA 3169036 - BI 4.2 and BI 4.3 - Fixed CVE and Vulnerabilities List

Always follow the SAP Patch Day every month (2nd Tuesday of the month)
The CVE fixes are published SAP Security Patch Day 

I recommend everyone to update the latest patch, since many critical CVEs, especially those with a "Trusted Authentication", have been remedied.

entry.title: Re: Layout characters limitation Business Objects 4.3
entry.content:
Solution:
Must make changes in the BigQuery.sbo file and increase the value of as required
<Parameter Name="Default Character Column Size">128</Parameter>

see KBA 3298025 - Cell text getting truncated after the 128 character length in WebIntelligence

entry.title: Re: i don't need the line no in my Web intelligence SAP BO report
entry.content: Add an example or screenshot to understand your problem and give you the possible correct answer

entry.title: Re: Field Value is not visible in SAP CAP List Report usi...
entry.content: if it is 1:n, how the framework knows which one to pickup?

entry.title: Re: Error 'No tax code found for difference' in Processin...
entry.content: Hello,   please share us how to solve the issue because we have the same issue

entry.title: IP30 doesn't reschedule maintenance plan as there is an order TECOed
entry.content:
Hi All:

We use IP30 mass schedule maintenance plan. 

We have maintenance order converted from call and TECOed, in order to let new planning follow up with planning key date but not TECO date, we change 2 "Shift Factor" in maintenance plan.

We tested the changes using IP30 in EAQ, maintenance plan is correctly planned at planning key date but not TECO date.

But as we made the change in PRD after a few of days, we found IP30 doesn't reschedule maintenance plan at planning key day but still on TECO date, however, IP10 can reschedule maintenance plan at planning key day but not on TECO date any more. And further at this time we go back to EAQ to test, we found IP10 can  reschedule maintenance plan at planning key day but not on TECO date any more, IP30 doesn't work longer.

Appreciate any sharing.

Yinyun

entry.title: Aps and WACS servers not starting
entry.content: We have been having some issues with the APS and WACS servers not starting. When we start them they just stay initializing. The only way we have remedy this is to re-install the entire BO. Is there any way to fix the servers without re-installing BO

entry.title: Created an iFlow to receive a file and send it to an SFTP server
entry.content:
Hi Everyone

I’ve created an iFlow in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) that is designed to receive a file and forward it to an SFTP server. The iFlow is deployed and seems to be working

 

When trying to test the API with CURL, I get an error.

curl -v -X POST https://xxxx-iflmap.hcisbp.us2.hana.ondemand.com/http/uploadSFTP -u s-user:pass -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" --data-binary "@archivo.txt"

Error: X-message-code: PWD_WRONG

Questions:

Can I use an s-user, or does it have to be a technical user?

What other possible causes could there be?

 

Thanks you

entry.title: Re: Cash Application with Machine Learning - summary info
entry.content:
Hello Ignacio,

I read your blog and its super useful, additionally read the comments from former_member683952, iona

I am working on SAP since 20 years and also worked on Cash App applications extensively, Lockbox with customized code to increase the match rate, High Radius, Esker, but all are more or less same in the hit ratio. In my opinion there is no perfect solution for this as there are too many moving parts. Our friend has raised the concerns on Cash App model by SAP but didn't provide any solution, did you connected with him offline and is there any solution to this business issue, if not then business has to pick some Cash App solution available in market as plain vanilla SAP process will not give any value to them.

entry.title: SAP EarlyWatch Alertについて
entry.content:
ご担当者様


お世話になっております。


 


SAP EarlyWatch Alertセッションからレポートを生成できる認識です。


こちらに対象の日付のものを出力することは可能でしょうか？


例えば、現在(5/20)のものがレポート生成できますが、5/1時点のレポートを生成する方法や手順があれば、


ご教授いただきたいです。


 


以上、よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP EarlyWatch Alertについて
entry.content:
KBA 1829914 - Customize EWA Reports - SAP Solution Manager によりますと、

EWAは1週間に1回のみスケジュールでき、前の週の月曜日から日曜日までのデータを提供します。EWAを生成するデフォルトの日は月曜日です。この開始日をトランザクションSOLMAN_EWA_ADMINを使用して変更することができます。トランザクションSOLMAN_EWA_ADMINで日を変更しても、EWAレポートの週の範囲は変更されないことに注意してください。日の設定に関係なく、EWAは常に月曜日から日曜日の範囲でEWAレポートを生成します。

つまり、EWAがデフォルトで生成する時間を変更したい場合、例えば毎週月曜日から毎週火曜日に変更することは可能です。

しかしお客様のの説明によれば、5月20日に生成されたEWAを5月1日に生成するように変更したいようですが、EWAは前の週の月曜日から日曜日までのデータを提供しますからそれは不可能です。

5月20日に生成されたEWAは、5月12日から18日までのデータを収集していますが、5月1日に生成されたEWAは4月21日から27日までのデータを収集します。

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Cloud - Error Logs
entry.content: Hi - Did you receive the answer to your above query ? I see only the Apps available to view the logs but no option to read them via API's or any option to enable the notifications

entry.title: How to un-install Simple Diagnostic Agent (SDA) -- Focused Run
entry.content:
To un-install SDA, please follow steps below:

1. Execute ps -fu sapadm on OS level. 

2. Stop all java processes of 'sapadm' user, Stop all /bin/sh ./go.sh processes of user 'sapadm'.  

3. For Windows:
    Delete the directors\files below:
    C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\SMDAgent
    C:\Program Files\SAP\hostctrl\exe\descriptors.d\SimpleDA.lmsldesc 
    For Linux:
    Delete the directors\files below:
    /usr/sap/hostctrl/SMDAgent/
    /usr/sap/hostctrl/exe/descriptors.d/SimpleDA.lmsldesc

4. Restart the SAP Host Agent. 

In case re-install SDA needed, please execute the Mass Action "Install/Update Agent" in the Agent Management Application (Agent Admin UI).

entry.title: Re: Regarding Fixed Asset Depreciation Areas
entry.content: Dear Jeremy, Your assistance was invaluable.I really appreciate your patient support! Without your expertise, I couldn't have solved the problem! I truly appreciate your help once again! Best regards,Yahaha

entry.title: Add a percentage to freight on an AR invoice created by the document generation wizard prior to add
entry.content:
My client needs to add a percentage markup to freight on an AR invoice prior to the document being added. The percentage markup would only apply to a specific set of customers.
My issue is these invoices are either created by document generation wizard, or imported. 
Does anyone know if it’s possible to catch the document add from the document generation wizard prior to actually adding the document?

Is there a way to call a program from transaction notification in Hana? I know this can be done in SQL as I have done it before but I don’t see a way to do it in Hana.

The client is running SAP business one version 10 for Hana

Any assistance you can provide would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content: Hi RaymondGiuseppi,

entry.title: BYD2505 GL Trial Balance report issue
entry.content:
how to show reporting structure Hierarchy for field G/L Account in the some reports, eg Trial Balance


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2505 GL Trial Balance report issue
entry.content:
• Open the Report: Trial Balance, maintain the need data and run the report
  • Then, navigate to view， Click on the three dots to the right of field  G/L Accounts
  • Click characteristic-->Hierarchy section, here you can maintain Display As Hierarchy and the Initial Expansion Level to achieve your expectation

entry.title: Re: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content:
Hi RaymondGiuseppi, wonder if you know the post you share cannot reach to the KBA:2745441.
It said the page has been removed. Just let you know, Thanks for your answer.

entry.title: Re: While debugging in Eclipse, I get an error “no author...
entry.content:
The cause was found.
When implemented from a Public Cloud custom logic app, the ABAP language version is for the key user.
Eclipse does not support value editing during debugging with this language version.
If you want to edit values during debugging, you need to do a BAdI implementation within Eclipse.

entry.title: Is there an extra cost to use SAP Customer Data Cloud for Recruiting?
entry.content: Is there a cost to use SAP Customer Data Cloud for Recruiting? Is there a benefit? I enabled it in my test system as soon as it was available and ran into many issues.  I've revisited it in my test system and it seems they've worked most of the issues out, although there are still some - it appears useable at this point.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | APAC | English
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with #SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: How to fetch Country of Origin from MARA to MCH1 in b...

entry.title: Re: SAP Table for Budget Report
entry.content: Did you execute an SQL trace during execution of S_ALR_87013558 ?

entry.title: Re: Entry is no longer valid (check the input)
entry.content:
To address the issue related to deactivating storage locations in SAP S/4HANA, I recommend referring to the following SAP Community Blog:
How To Deactivate Storage Location In S/4 HANA 
This allows you to control the use of a storage location without affecting other plants. The article also discusses the implications of these settings on material master data and related transactions.

I hope this helps! Let me know your thoughts or if you have any questions.
Thanks,

Akshay Anil   Linkedin Profile
Technical Consultant, SAP ABAP | BTP | Fiori | Ui5

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Asset accounting - units-produced depreciation key
entry.content:
Hello. I am implementing fixed asset accounting in SAP S4 HANA Cloud for a company in Mexico. This company requires the following areas to exist in the chart of depreciation:

Area 01: This is the main area that posts in real time to Ledger 0L with accounting principle 60 and a straight-line depreciation key.

Area 02: This area does not post and is assigned to Ledger 0L with accounting principle 60. It calculates depreciation for the Machinery and Equipment fixed asset classes using the units-produced depreciation method (key).

Has anyone implemented Area 02? Could you help me with the step-by-step configuration or a manual?

I would greatly appreciate it.

entry.title: Re: How to consume a RAP Behavior Event for R_JOURNAL- SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition ( ABAP CLOUD
entry.content: Removed general tag.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | EMEA | NA | LAC | English

Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with #SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document Management ISO:27001/GoBD/DSGVO German Standards
entry.content: Removed general tag.

entry.title: Asset accounting - tax valuation area
entry.content:
Hello. I am implementing fixed asset accounting in SAP S4 HANA Cloud for a company in Mexico. This company requires the following areas to exist in the chart of depreciation:

Area 01: This is the main area that posts in real time to Ledger 0L with accounting principle 60 and a straight-line depreciation key.

Area 05: This is a non-posting tax valuation area assigned to Ledger 0L with accounting principle 60. It calculates fixed asset depreciation according to Mexican tax regulations and takes into account the inflation rate for each period.

Has anyone implemented Area 05? Could you help me with the step-by-step configuration or a manual?

I would greatly appreciate it.

entry.title: 999 storage type difference posting for sto
entry.content:
Hi Experts Please suggest ....

SAP WM managed system .

We are creating Replenishment sto from DC to store via sto order and when ever To is created for outbound delivery many items have short pick and nill pick and all the difference is accumulated in 999 storage type the number is very huge because many stores and many orders . So is there a way to avoid this or to  automatically knock off ?? Please suggest

This occurs for Physical inventory count as well .

entry.title: Re: Bi weekly/Weekly Notifications for pending Time Sheet in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hi @harinich - If the creating and sending notification works fine manually from manage data, then it should work the same way via IC. IC essentially calls the create or update of MDF. I would suggest to trigger the MDF creation via IC with rule trace. Also check if the IC operation is set correctly to "UPSERT SINGLE" and not others.  If IC is not able to create/update the MDF then you need to redo /check the IC config

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL Change output file content in create Golden...
entry.content:
hello @Yong_Yu 

My customer want to change output file layout. they want to add some field in the output file .

can you tell me someplace where can fullfill this request?

entry.title: BTP Workzone (standard) update config to allow additional origin
entry.content:
Hi,

Anyone has idea how to configure BTP/BTP Workzone to allow additional origin called from the third-party URL?

Scenario below: Using OCI catalog punch out scenario -> that Workzone hosted the S/4 Standard App- Webdynpro Change maintenance order App will launch a third-party catalog in a new tab when user click on select catalog button in the component screen, and the third-party catalog URL will post the selected data back to SAP via hook URL maintained in the configuration in table WSI_CALLSTRUC.

The hook URL we have maintained the BTP Workzone backend destination URL: for example:
https://<subaccount-host>-sapdelim-s4d05rt500.launchpad.cfapps.ca10.hana.ondemand.com/sap/bc/rest/sap/oci_catalog

where this URL is where we observe how the workzone route other odata Call from workzone to backend S/4.

We are getting the error below from the BTP response after user click submit the cart in the catalog page that trigger to the HOOK_URL.

Note: We found out this error is responded by the BTP, not by the local browser, hence something needs to be done in BTP to allow this origin, not the normal CORS blocked by the common browser scenario.

What we found out further due to the third party will set the HTTP request header "Origin":"https://www.grainger.ca" in the hook URL request that is causing the issue where blocked by BTP/BTP workzone.

We have try simulate the same request from postman, able to get the same error. However, once we remove the Orgin from the HTTP header, it is able to post to the backend with 200 error success.

So far found this note 3169523 - Configure Tenant Security Header for CAI - SAP for Me that is allow us to configure additional origin, but is applicable for CAI only, not for Workzone.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Customer projects S...

entry.title: Re: AIF customizing screen not allowing to use /TMINT nam...
entry.content: SAP On Premise, right? You may Unlock the namespace using transaction /AIF/NS_LOCK. And you have to understand '/' is reversed for SAP https://me.sap.com/notes/3071632/E.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL Change output file content in create Golden...
entry.content: Currently, the output fields can't be changed. Please try to reuse the as-is unused fields to put customer's contents.

entry.title: Re: Problem to execute Examples in "Solving Your Business...
entry.content:
Hi @Nobue 

As error is clearly mentioned "tiiuae--falcon-40b-instruct" deployment doesn't exist.

you will have to try another AI model which is deployed and update it in your python code

entry.title: Re: Simulation option missing in SAP Enable Now Producer
entry.content:
Hi @Amira_303 ,

It looks like the Resources weren't synced 100% in the Producer for the "problematic" workspace. This workspace is not System, right? Because this is the only workspace that is not intended for content development, and hence doesn't have required resources.

Check in the Manager whether the resources exist and their quantity is similar to resources in other workspaces. If true, check them out in the Producer. 

If resources in Manager are missing, then run the Revert resources operation in Manager. It will restore the missing resources.

entry.title: Re: Rebilling of ordered items via customer project
entry.content: When you say "recharge to customer", what exactly are your requirements?

entry.title: Re: Web & Mobile App Development Option is not available...
entry.content:
HI @alokraj002hbl 

When you select Create New Project ---> Application -- you select Frontend (As shown in Screenshot) - Web and Mobile Application

entry.title: Re: SCORM content in LMS not accessible
entry.content:
Try the SCORM package in a testing tool like the cloud.scorm.com. if it works there, then it might be the customer's LMS system issue, and the browser debugger tools will be your best friend to find out what exactly the LMS doesn't like 

If the package also fails in the cloud.scorm.com, then there is something wrong with the content or settings. I've been exporting SAP Enables Now content to SCORM for years, and can confirm without a doubt that books with sims work fine.

entry.title: Re: Batch-specific / -neutral available quantity in storage type when picking for supply to producit
entry.content: @castin - In this case you would have to manually use the button to trigger an HU withdrawal. Please check this.

entry.title: Re: How to Redirect SAP CC Backoffice to Custom Domain
entry.content:
By following the instructions in the Creating a Website Redirect document, you can redirect the root domain to the /backoffice path. The following directive should work when added to the redirect file:

RedirectMatch 302 "^/$" "/backoffice"

You can change the redirect status code to 301 if you want the redirection to be considered permanent.

entry.title: Re: Integration of custom fields in standard Master Data Object using ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT prog

Hi,

I am facing challenge in assigning the admin role to business user in S/4HANA public cloud practical test in learning journey of (SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). I am selecting mange business user tile but nothing is happening. could any one he

entry.title: Build Process Automation fails only to send emails within process execution
entry.content:
I have added a process to send an email within a BPA process.

On the BTP Cockpit, we have set Destination to an external SMTP mail service (using Amazon SES), and test mail sending from [Control Tower] -> [Mail Server] in BPA is sending mail without problems.

When I send an email in the process, I am getting an authentication error, but I don't know how to resolve it.
Does the handling of authentication information in Destination change in the process?

Could not send email: The server 'XXXXXXXXXXXXX.amazonaws.com' could be reached, but the login with the given credentials failed. The server has returned the following message: '535 Authentication Credentials Invalid'.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Transformation Center: Digital Blueprint creation.
entry.content:
Hi @Yogananda 

Changes have been made, Thanks

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting- Data Migration & Cutover Approach
entry.content:
Hi massarrat_unwala,

there is no easy answer to it.
With option 2 you can test the conso process and if everything works as planned and you would be able to compare the results against the previous close - especially when no reporting will happen in the old conso tool in April 26.

Option 1 could be quicker,

depending on granularity by doc.typs, you need to consider if doc.types should be reversed or not

- auto. doc.types can not be easily used for Upload so you need to make sure to align it to the migration process (Monitor settings/doc type settings (re-using Auto. Docs or create separate migration docs.), etc.) and changing settings between migration and go live could be tricky (especially, when you are not finished in time with the migration) and could have a negative impact, if you miss something to set back in time.

From my pov the safest way is option 2

I hope this helps

Stephan

entry.title: Secondary Data Determination Error in CG02
entry.content: After the Expert Rules update procedure, the following Error was displayed when I click Secondary Data Determination in CG02. Could you help me analyze the cause of this error? Thank you.

entry.title: Aging List report to display overdue amount after currency revaluation
entry.content:
Hello all,

Our company uses 2 FX rates - 

1. Default rate which is used for daily transactions

2. Monthly Average Exchange Rate which is the closing rate for the last day of the month.

The Aging list reports only show the overdue amount using the Default rates but the GL amount is reporting the amount after currency revaluation.

I saw in another forum, we need to use the Accounts Receivable - Open Items report which shows the Amount using Exchange Rate from Foreign Currency Remeasurement of Receivables while Aging List for Receivables report shows the Amount with the Default Exchange Rate.

I would like to know if we can set up the Aging reports to show the amount after Currency Remeasurement?

We need the Aging reports as our auditors are requesting for it.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SPED output type error
entry.content:
Hi,

             We checked in ST22, its happened because of custom development. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: 999 storage type difference posting for sto
entry.content:
Hello @ab20468864 

WM movements don't change overall stock levels. Transfer orders just move stock between storage bins, physical or virtual/intermediate ones. 999 is a virtual storage type where stock differences are posted. The differences are created when transfer orders are confirmed with differences or during physical inventory process.

The stock differences need to be cleared periodically with Clearing Differences process. You are missing this step.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP Deployment-Functional Modules
entry.content:
1.On premises.

2.Cloud-i)Public ii)Private

3.Hybrid -Combination of on premises and cloud.

Offerings from SAP 

1.Rise with SAP -Elevating Transformation-Recommended for larger size and larger mid market organization in size.

2.Grow with SAP-Nuturing Business Expansion-Tailored for Small,medium size oraganizations.

3.SAP S/4 HANA -Enpowering Core Operations-Flagship,Next generation ERP Suite-Offers real time insights ,Simplified Workflows-Modern UX.

entry.title: Re: How to update the 3E Regulation Content in EHS？
entry.content: Hello C.B. Thank you for your reply. This update is indeed a complex process, and I am updating the ERC according to the data package and documents provided by 3E.

entry.title: Storage Section Check
entry.content:
Hi SAP-Community,

We would like to set up two storage sections (A, B) for one storage type. When creating a putaway warehouse task, the storage type and storage section should be taken into account so that the storage section is displayed in the RFUI during putaway.

The requirement is that a storage bin in storage section B can also be selected despite storage section A being specified. Is this possible without an exception code?

The setting for the Storage Section Check is Y (Storage Section Determination; No Check). I would have expected that this setting allows this. When selecting a storage bin in Storage Section B, we run into the error message "E: Storage bin "" is not assigned to storage section" ( /SCWM/L3088)

In the Storage Section Search Sequence we maintained both A and B (First A, then B) for Storage Section Indicator A. 

What is the difference between Storage Section Check Y and X?

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 Binded Text inside a ScrollContainer
entry.content: Thank you! This approach seems to work just fine! 🙂

entry.title: Re: variable substitution, filename - remove file extension
Re: No Currency Pair is defined for currencies
entry.content:
Currency exchange rates for the currency pair EUR-USD is defined in the App Currency Exchange Rate

the currency exchange rates can be created and deleted from the app. Also, see what is Direct and Indirect Exchange Rates

For automatic update of exchange rate the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system use the Market Datafeed

Currency notation can be defined in the App Assign Currency Notations - Datafeed

Follow the Set-up instruction for datafeed

Use the App Request Currency Market Data to retrieve the market data into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

Please, feel free to reach out to SAP Support with a Support ticket if you are still facing any issue.

 

Kind regards,

Bhawesh

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Changing custom table delivery class from A to C
entry.content: No, it won't delete any existing entries.

entry.title: Developing a UI5 App with Value Help from CSV, Charts with VizFrame, and Excel Upload/Download
entry.content:
SAPUI5 (SAP User Interface for HTML5) is a JavaScript-based UI framework developed by SAP. It is used for building responsive, cross-platform web applications that run in desktop and mobile browsers. SAPUI5 is the foundation of SAP Fiori, which is SAP’s design language and user experience strategy.

Key Features:

  • MVC Architecture: Supports the Model-View-Controller pattern for clean code separation.

  • Rich UI Controls: Includes a comprehensive set of UI controls optimized for business applications.

  • Data Binding: Supports multiple data models (JSON, XML, OData) for dynamic content rendering.

  • Responsive Design: Adapts to different screen sizes and devices automatically.

  • Theming and Customization: Allows theming via the SAP Theme Designer.

 

What is BAS (Business Application Studio)?

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) is a modern development environment provided by SAP. It's designed for building business applications, especially those based on SAP technologies like SAPUI5, SAP Fiori, CAP (Cloud Application Programming model), and more.

Think of BAS as SAP's version of Visual Studio Code — but hosted in the cloud and optimized for enterprise application development.

A Dev Space in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) is a pre-configured, isolated development environment tailored for specific types of SAP development — like SAPUI5, CAP, or Fiori.

 

What this app does – simplified scenarios:

  • Show a page titled "Dummy App" with a clean, centered header.

  • Collect user input using a form with the following fields:

    • Name input field with a value help dialog (e.g., a search or selection help).

    • Age input field, also with value help.

    • Company input field, again with value help.

  • Let users submit the form by clicking the "Submit" button, which likely stores or processes the entered data.

  • Display a small bar chart:

    • Shows the number of entries (Count) grouped by Age.

    • Useful for visualizing how many users fall into each age group.

  • Provide a results table that displays the submitted data:

    • Each row shows a person's Name, Age, and Company.

    • Table grows dynamically to show more entries as needed.

  • Allow data export and import:

    • "Download" button lets users export the table data (likely to Excel).

    • "Upload" button lets users import data from a file (probably Excel or CSV).

 

View1.view.xml :

<mvc:View
    controllerName="com.sap.btp.project1.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:viz="sap.viz.ui5.controls"
    xmlns:viz.data="sap.viz.ui5.data"
    xmlns:viz.feeds="sap.viz.ui5.controls.common.feeds"
    xmlns:export="sap.ui.export">

    <!-- ✅ Wrap Page inside App to enable scrolling properly -->
    <App>
        <Page id="page" showHeader="false">
            <ScrollContainer height="100%" vertical="true" focusable="true">
                <VBox class="sapUiMediumMargin">

                    <!-- 🔵 App Header -->
                    <VBox class="appHeader sapUiMediumMargin" alignItems="Center">
                        <Text text="Dummy App" textAlign="Center" class="appTitle" />
                    </VBox>

                    <!-- 🔶 Main Layout -->
                    <HBox wrap="Wrap">

                        <!-- 🔹 Form with Value Help Inputs -->
<VBox class="formContainer sapUiSmallMarginEnd">

    <!-- 🔹 Name Input with Value Help -->
<HBox class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom" alignItems="Center">
    <Label text="Name :" width="100px"/>
    <Input id="nameInput"
           width="300px"
           showValueHelp="true"
           value="{/name}"
           valueHelpRequest="onNameValueHelp" />
</HBox>


   <!-- 🔹 Age -->
<HBox class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom" alignItems="Center">
    <Label text="Age :" width="100px"/>
    <Input id="ageInput"
           width="300px"
           showValueHelp="true"
           value="{/age}"
           valueHelpRequest="onAgeValueHelp" />
</HBox>

<!-- 🔹 Company -->
<HBox class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom" alignItems="Center">
    <Label text="Company :" width="100px"/>
    <Input id="companyInput"
           width="300px"
           showValueHelp="true"
           value="{/company}"
           valueHelpRequest="onCompanyValueHelp" />
</HBox>


    <Button text="Submit" press="onSubmit" type="Emphasized" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />
</VBox>


                        <!-- 📊 Smaller Chart -->
                        <VBox>
                            <viz:VizFrame id="ageChart"
                                          height="250px"
                                          width="400px"
                                          uiConfig="{applicationSet:'fiori'}"
                                          vizType="column">
                                <viz:dataset>
                                    <viz.data:FlattenedDataset data="{/ageChartData}">
                                        <viz.data:dimensions>
                                            <viz.data:DimensionDefinition name="Age" value="{age}" />
                                        </viz.data:dimensions>
                                        <viz.data:measures>
                                            <viz.data:MeasureDefinition name="Count" value="{count}" />
                                        </viz.data:measures>
                                    </viz.data:FlattenedDataset>
                                </viz:dataset>
                                <viz:feeds>
                                    <viz.feeds:FeedItem uid="valueAxis" type="Measure" values="Count"/>
                                    <viz.feeds:FeedItem uid="categoryAxis" type="Dimension" values="Age"/>
                                </viz:feeds>
                            </viz:VizFrame>
                        </VBox>
                    </HBox>

                    <!-- 🟢 Table & Buttons -->
                    <VBox class="sapUiLargeMarginTop sapUiResponsiveMargin">
                        <HBox justifyContent="End">
                            <Button text="Download"
                                    press="onDownloadExcel"
                                    type="Emphasized"
                                    class="sapUiMediumMarginTop sapUiSmallMarginEnd"/>

                            <Button text="Upload"
                                    press="onUploadFile"
                                    type="Emphasized"
                                    class="sapUiMediumMarginTop"/>
                        </HBox>

                        <Table id="resultTable" items="{/submittedData}" width="100%" growing="true" growingThreshold="20">
                            <columns>
                                <Column><Text text="Name" /></Column>
                                <Column><Text text="Age" /></Column>
                                <Column><Text text="Company" /></Column>
                            </columns>
                            <items>
                                <ColumnListItem>
                                    <cells>
                                        <Text text="{name}" />
                                        <Text text="{age}" />
                                        <Text text="{company}" />
                                    </cells>
                                </ColumnListItem>
                            </items>
                        </Table>
                    </VBox>

                </VBox>
            </ScrollContainer>
        </Page>
    </App>
</mvc:View>
 
XML Namespaces (in SAPUI5, they are often referred to as "UI5 Namespaces").

In an XML-based SAPUI5 view (like View.xml), XML namespaces define which library or module each tag or control belongs to. They prevent naming conflicts and allow the use of multiple libraries in one file.

 

  • xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    ➤ Enables use of MVC tags like <mvc:View> for defining views and controllers.

  • xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    ➤ Provides access to core UI5 controls and features like event handling, formatting, etc.

  • xmlns="sap.m"
    ➤ Default namespace for mobile-ready UI controls like Button, Input, VBox, etc.

  • xmlns:viz="sap.viz.ui5.controls"
    ➤ Gives access to VizFrame chart controls for data visualization.

  • xmlns:viz.data="sap.viz.ui5.data"
    ➤ Used to define datasets, dimensions, and measures for VizFrame charts.

  • xmlns:viz.feeds="sap.viz.ui5.controls.common.feeds"
    ➤ Maps data to chart axes (e.g. valueAxis, categoryAxis) in VizFrame.

  • xmlns:export="sap.ui.export"
    ➤ Provides access to export utilities like Spreadsheet for downloading Excel files.

 

 

Brief Summary of the SAPUI5 View

This SAPUI5 XML View defines a simple Fiori-style web app UI that includes:

  • 🏷️ Header: Displays the title “Dummy App” at the top.

  • 📝Form Section: Allows the user to enter Name, Age, and Company using input fields with value help dialogs (for selection support).

  • 📊Chart: Shows a column chart that visualizes Age-wise count data, using sap.viz.ui5.controls.VizFrame.

  • 📄Data Table: Displays submitted form entries (Name, Age, Company) in a growing table.

  • 📥📤Buttons:

    • "Submit" to send form data.

    • "Download" to export the table (e.g., to Excel).

    • "Upload" to import data (e.g., from a file).

View1.controller.js:

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageBox",
    "sap/viz/ui5/controls/VizFrame",
    "sap/viz/ui5/data/FlattenedDataset",
    "sap/viz/ui5/data/DimensionDefinition",
    "sap/viz/ui5/data/MeasureDefinition",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
    "sap/ui/export/Spreadsheet",
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    "sap/m/SelectDialog",
    "sap/m/StandardListItem",
    "sap/ui/model/Filter",
    "sap/ui/model/FilterOperator"
], function (
    Controller,
    MessageBox,
    VizFrame,
    FlattenedDataset,
    DimensionDefinition,
    MeasureDefinition,
    JSONModel,
    Spreadsheet,
    MessageToast,
    SelectDialog,
    StandardListItem,
    Filter,
    FilterOperator
) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("com.sap.btp.project1.controller.View1", {
        onInit: function () {
            var oView = this.getView();
    var oModel = new JSONModel({
        name: "",
        age: "",
        company: "",
        dropdownData: {
            names: [],
            ages: [],
            companies: []
        },
        submittedData: [],
        ageChartData: []
    });
    oView.setModel(oModel);

            var sCSVPath = jQuery.sap.getModulePath("com.sap.btp.project1", "/model/dropdown_master.csv");

            jQuery.ajax({
                url: sCSVPath,
                dataType: "text",
                success: function (data) {
                    var lines = data.split("\n").filter(Boolean);
                    var names = [], ages = [], companies = [];
                    var ageCountMap = {};

                    for (var i = 1; i < lines.length; i++) {
                        var values = lines[i].split(",");
                        if (values.length < 3) continue;

                        var nameVal = values[0].trim();
                        var ageVal = values[1].trim();
                        var companyVal = values[2].trim();

                        names.push({ key: nameVal, text: nameVal });
                        ages.push({ key: ageVal, text: ageVal });
                        companies.push({ key: companyVal, text: companyVal });

                        if (ageVal) {
                            ageCountMap[ageVal] = (ageCountMap[ageVal] || 0) + 1;
                        }
                    }

                    var ageChartData = Object.keys(ageCountMap).map(function (age) {
                        return {
                            age: age,
                            count: ageCountMap[age]
                        };
                    });

                    oModel.setProperty("/dropdownData/names", names);
                    oModel.setProperty("/dropdownData/ages", ages);
                    oModel.setProperty("/dropdownData/companies", companies);
                    oModel.setProperty("/ageChartData", ageChartData);
                },
                error: function () {
                    MessageToast.show("Failed to load dropdown CSV data.");
                }
            });
        },

        // Value Help for Name
        onNameValueHelp: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aNames = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/names");

            var oDialog = new SelectDialog({
                title: "Select Name",
                items: aNames.map(function (item) {
                    return new StandardListItem({ title: item.text });
                }),
                liveChange: function (oEvent) {
                    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
                    var oFilter = new Filter("title", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
                    oDialog.getBinding("items").filter([oFilter]);
                },
                confirm: function (oEvent) {
                    var selected = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                    if (selected) {
                        this.getView().byId("nameInput").setValue(selected.getTitle());
                    }
                }.bind(this)
            });

            oDialog.open();
        },

        // Value Help for Age
        onAgeValueHelp: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aAges = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/ages");

            var oDialog = new SelectDialog({
                title: "Select Age",
                items: aAges.map(function (item) {
                    return new StandardListItem({ title: item.text });
                }),
                liveChange: function (oEvent) {
                    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
                    var oFilter = new Filter("title", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
                    oDialog.getBinding("items").filter([oFilter]);
                },
                confirm: function (oEvent) {
                    var selected = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                    if (selected) {
                        this.getView().byId("ageInput").setValue(selected.getTitle());
                    }
                }.bind(this)
            });

            oDialog.open();
        },

        // Value Help for Company
        onCompanyValueHelp: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aCompanies = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/companies");

            var oDialog = new SelectDialog({
                title: "Select Company",
                items: aCompanies.map(function (item) {
                    return new StandardListItem({ title: item.text });
                }),
                liveChange: function (oEvent) {
                    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
                    var oFilter = new Filter("title", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
                    oDialog.getBinding("items").filter([oFilter]);
                },
                confirm: function (oEvent) {
                    var selected = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                    if (selected) {
                        this.getView().byId("companyInput").setValue(selected.getTitle());
                    }
                }.bind(this)
            });

            oDialog.open();
        },

        onSubmit: function () {
            var oView = this.getView();
            var name = oView.byId("nameInput").getValue();
            var age = oView.byId("ageInput").getValue();
            var company = oView.byId("companyInput").getValue();
       
            var oModel = oView.getModel();
           
            // Make sure these values are set to the model
            oModel.setProperty("/name", name);
            oModel.setProperty("/age", age);
            oModel.setProperty("/company", company);
       
            var validAges = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/ages").map(a => a.text);
            var validCompanies = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/companies").map(c => c.text);
       
            if (!age || !validAges.includes(age)) {
                MessageBox.error("Please select a valid Age.");
                return;
            }
            if (!company || !validCompanies.includes(company)) {
                MessageBox.error("Please select a valid Company.");
                return;
            }
       
            var message = `Name: ${name}\nAge: ${age}\nCompany: ${company}`;
            MessageBox.information(message, { title: "Submitted Details" });
        },
       

       
        onDownloadExcel: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aData = oModel.getProperty("/submittedData");

            var oExportData = aData.map(function (item) {
                return {
                    "Name": item.name,
                    "Age": item.age,
                    "Company": item.company
                };
            });

            var oSpreadsheet = new Spreadsheet({
                workbook: {
                    columns: [
                        { label: 'Name', property: 'Name' },
                        { label: 'Age', property: 'Age' },
                        { label: 'Company', property: 'Company' }
                    ]
                },
                dataSource: oExportData,
                fileName: "table_data.xlsx"
            });

            oSpreadsheet.build().then(function () {
                MessageToast.show("Excel file has been downloaded!");
            });
        },

        onUploadFile: function () {
            var oInputFile = document.createElement("input");
            oInputFile.type = "file";
            oInputFile.accept = ".xlsx, .xls";
            oInputFile.onchange = this._onFileSelected.bind(this);
            oInputFile.click();
        },

        _onFileSelected: function (oEvent) {
            var oFile = oEvent.target.files[0];

            if (!oFile) {
                MessageToast.show("No file selected.");
                return;
            }

            var loadXLSX = function () {
                return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
                    if (typeof XLSX !== "undefined") {
                        resolve();
                        return;
                    }

                    var script = document.createElement("script");
                    script.src = jQuery.sap.getModulePath("com.sap.btp.project1") + "/libs/xlsx.full.min.js";
                    script.onload = resolve;
                    script.onerror = function () {
                        reject(new Error("Failed to load XLSX library."));
                    };
                    document.head.appendChild(script);
                });
            };

            loadXLSX().then(function () {
                var reader = new FileReader();

                reader.onload = function (e) {
                    try {
                        var data = new Uint8Array(e.target.result);
                        var workbook = XLSX.read(data, { type: 'array' });

                        var firstSheetName = workbook.SheetNames[0];
                        var worksheet = workbook.Sheets[firstSheetName];

                        var jsonData = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(worksheet);
                        var cleanedData = jsonData.map(function (item) {
                            return {
                                name: item.Name || "",
                                age: item.Age || "",
                                company: item.Company || ""
                            };
                        });

                        var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
                        oModel.setProperty("/submittedData", cleanedData);

                        MessageToast.show("Excel data loaded successfully.");
                    } catch (err) {
                        console.error("Excel parsing error:", err);
                        MessageToast.show("Failed to read Excel file.");
                    }
                }.bind(this);

                reader.readAsArrayBuffer(oFile);
            }.bind(this)).catch(function (err) {
                console.error(err);
                MessageToast.show("Could not load XLSX library.");
            });
        }
    });
});
 

 

SAPUI5 Module Imports & Their Purpose

"sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
🔹Base class for all controllers. Used to define your custom controller logic.
"sap/m/MessageBox"
🔹Used to show popup dialog boxes (e.g., for errors, confirmations, info messages).
"sap/viz/ui5/controls/VizFrame"
🔹Core control for rendering charts (bar, pie, line, etc.) in SAPUI5 apps.
"sap/viz/ui5/data/FlattenedDataset"
🔹Defines the data format for charts, mapping flat JSON data to visual elements.
"sap/viz/ui5/data/DimensionDefinition"
🔹Specifies dimensions (e.g., Age) used on the x-axis or category axis of the chart.
"sap/viz/ui5/data/MeasureDefinition"
🔹Specifies measures (e.g., Count) used on the y-axis or value axis of the chart.
"sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
🔹Provides a client-side data model using JSON, used to bind data to the UI.
"sap/ui/export/Spreadsheet"
🔹Used for exporting table data to Excel (.xlsx) format.
"sap/m/MessageToast"
🔹Displays temporary messages (toasts) at the bottom of the screen (e.g., “Excel downloaded!”).
"sap/m/SelectDialog"
🔹A searchable dialog used in value help (dropdown) selections.
"sap/m/StandardListItem"
🔹UI element representing a standard item in a list, used inside SelectDialog.
"sap/ui/model/Filter"
🔹Enables filtering of items (e.g., in the value help dialog based on user input).
"sap/ui/model/FilterOperator"
🔹Provides filter operations like Contains, EQ, etc., for filtering logic.
 

✅Key Functionalities Explained Briefly:

  1. onInit() – Load CSV Data:

    • Loads dropdown_master.csv from the model folder.

    • Extracts Name, Age, and Company options.

    • Stores the values in a JSON model under /dropdownData.

    • Also generates chart data (/ageChartData) based on age counts.

  2. 🔍Value Help (SelectDialog) for Each Field:

    • onNameValueHelp(), onAgeValueHelp(), onCompanyValueHelp() open a dialog with search.

    • Items are fetched from the loaded CSV data (/dropdownData).

    • The selected item is set in the respective input field.

  3. 📥onSubmit() – Handle Form Submission:

    • Validates that Age and Company are from valid lists.

    • Shows a message box with the entered Name, Age, and Company.

  4. 📤onDownloadExcel() – Export to Excel:

    • Downloads the /submittedData model (form entries) as an Excel .xlsx file.

  5. 📥onUploadFile() – Upload Excel File:

    • Lets the user upload an Excel file.

    • Reads and parses it using the XLSX library.

    • Updates /submittedData in the model, populating the table in the UI.

 

 

The data shown in the value help comes from a CSV file named dropdown_master.csv, which is located in the model folder inside the webapp directory. This CSV file is loaded as a model and used to populate the dropdown options in the value help dialog.

 

 
In the app, when you click on the value help  icon, a popup dialog opens displaying a list of selectable options. These options—for Name, Age, and Company—are all loaded from a CSV file (dropdown_master.csv) located in the model folder. The same logic applies to each input field.
 
 
 

 

I’ve included a GIF in the demo to visually showcase how the app functions.

 

Learning Materials:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-sapui5-applications 

Learning SAPUI5 for Beginners - SAP Community

Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

SAP Tutorial Navigator | Tutorials for SAP Developers

entry.title: Re: How to update the in house production time in material master work scheduling view ?
entry.content: Could you please provide an update or let me know if any changes are expected in the near future?

entry.title: Re: SAP Document Management ISO:27001/GoBD/DSGVO German Standards
entry.content: Asked SAP the same (1 year ago) and got no answer yet - we start to observe dedicated archive solutions in order to store attached documents to SAP objects via archivelink/CMIS in an archive solution which fulfill these kind of general requirement (plus GxP/ GMP).

entry.title: Block GRPO if Not for A specific Warehouse and A specific Bin
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to create a TN which blocks the addition of GRPO if a specific Bin location is not selected.

I have tried the below TN but it works only for warehouse but not for Bin:

DECLARE @Item AS NVARCHAR(25)
SET @Item = (SELECT TOP 1 ISNULL(T1.ItemCode,'')
FROM OPDN T0
INNER JOIN PDN1 T1 ON T0.DocEntry=T1.DocEntry
INNER JOIN OITM T2 ON T2.ItemCode = T1.ItemCode
INNER JOIN OILM T4 on T1.docentry=T4.DocEntry and T1.LineNum=T4.DocLineNum and T4.TransType=20 and T4.LocCode=T1.WhsCode --AND T4.AccumType=1
LEFT JOIN OBTL T5 on T4.MessageID= T5.MessageID
LEFT JOIN OBIN T6 on T5.BinAbs=T6.absentry and T6.whscode=T1.WhsCode
WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del AND T2.QryGroup5 = 'Y'
AND T1.WhsCode NOT IN ('50-QCH') AND T6.BinCode NOT IN( '50-QCH-QC')
)
IF (SELECT @Item) != ''
BEGIN
SET @error = 2003
SET @error_message = (SELECT @Item + ' Item is flagged for Review and must be moved to 50-QCH-QC Bin!!')
GOTO TN_RESULT
END

Any suggestions?

Regards,

Joseph

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Express Edition 2.00.076.00 License upgrade
entry.content:
Hello Sandip,

Please check following documentations:

  • 3247198 - SAP HANA License (and the See Also section)
  • SAP Help Portal - SAP HANA, express edition (Frequently Asked Questions)

Furthermore, same question already answered here:

  • SAP Hana Express licence with more than 32GB

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: Re: Web & Mobile App Development Option is not available...
entry.content: Thank You so much Yogananda.

entry.title: Re: Run Statutory Reports App custom billing document num...
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

The issue is likely due to the underlying data, not the Excel export format. If only a few billing documents show negative tax amounts, those documents probably involve credit memos, cancellations, or adjustments where the tax is legitimately negative in accounting.

Check the billing type and the related journal entries if the tax was posted as a credit (negative), Excel will just reflect that. It's not a formatting bug, but a reflection of how those specific transactions were processed.

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA: Best Practices for Future-Proof Extensions
entry.content:
No, clean core is way more nuanced, and no goal (or goal summary) in itself.

Side-by-side might be a good solution, for example, built on BTP or on a different (cheaper? better?) platform than BTP. However, that totally depends on the context. Many times I select an in-app(-core) extension for speed, cost and resilience (for example, when side-by-side requires extensive data syncing).

entry.title: Re: SAP Deployment-Functional Modules
entry.content:
Hi MVRAGHURAM,

This is a Q&A forum.

Can you please elaborate what is your question ? Do you need any details on deployment ?

If its not a question, please mark it as accepted answer and close it.

Thank you

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Data is showing in report preview but the report exported has no data - LMS
entry.content: Data is showing in report preview but the report exported has no data - LMS

entry.title: Re: Automated tender/RFP management for supplier - multin...
entry.content:
Great question — and you're definitely on the right track considering SAP Ariba.

To break it down:

  1. Finding Tenders/RFPs:
    SAP Ariba (now part of SAP Business Network) lets suppliers get matched with relevant tenders through what used to be called Ariba Discovery. That said, it's mostly buyer-driven, so visibility depends on how actively buyers post opportunities.

  2. Evaluating Opportunities:
    Ariba doesn't offer built-in scoring or evaluation tools for suppliers, but you can work around this by using your own internal tools or integrating it with your CRM or analytics platforms.

  3. Responding/Submitting:
    This is where Ariba works best. You can manage submissions, auto-fill standard info, collaborate internally, and track everything from one place. It's clean, compliant, and built for collaboration.

One thing to keep in mind: Ariba is designed with the buyer in mind. So, for full automation across all three steps, you might want to integrate Ariba with additional tools or workflows.

Happy to chat more if you’re exploring a solution design — this is right in my wheelhouse.
